mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate remains at the effective lower bound.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.12% indicates near-zero policy rate environment
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE=5.6% is at the trigger threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year, indicating weakening consumer demand that signals reduced corporate earnings growth. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth rate and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1% month-over-month, signaling strengthening manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity expansion that supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, indicating a synchronized contraction in consumer and construction demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the joint signal reflects broad-based demand weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=1028.0 thousand units, RSAFS=442931 million dollars
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 jobs per month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a labor market inflection point that triggers equity risk-off behavior and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50,000 AND UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of jointly-triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2%, creating a negative real rate environment that signals monetary policy lag. In a late-cycle expansion regime, this produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market anticipates future tightening.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the real rate signal does not predict forward returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling a late-cycle slowdown that reduces earnings growth expectations. This regime-conditional mechanism is active only in post-expansion periods and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth consensus revisions accelerate downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during post-expansion regimes, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 3-year window in late-cycle periods.
